# Transmission Remote GUI

> A free, open-source (MIT) native macOS remote GUI for the Transmission
> BitTorrent daemon. Built in SwiftUI for macOS 14+, it is a modern alternative
> to transgui (transmission-remote-gui), adding sequential streaming, RSS
> auto-download, labels/filters, live speed graphs, a bandwidth scheduler, and
> mTLS. Current version 0.1.4.

## Key facts
- Name: Transmission Remote GUI
- Platform: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later, Apple Silicon and Intel
- Price: Free (no paid tier, no subscription)
- License: MIT (open source)
- Built with: SwiftUI (native macOS); UI in English and Hungarian
- Category: BitTorrent remote-control client / Transmission RPC front-end
- Alternative to: transgui / transmission-remote-gui (classic cross-platform Lazarus app)
- Requires: a running Transmission daemon with RPC enabled (NAS, seedbox, Raspberry Pi, home server)
- Install: brew install --cask epaxpax/tap/transmission-remote-gui-macos, or download the .dmg

## Links
- Homepage: https://epaxpax.github.io/transmission-remote-gui/
- Source & releases: https://github.com/epaxpax/transmission-remote-gui
- Latest .dmg: https://github.com/epaxpax/transmission-remote-gui/releases/latest
- License: https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

## Features
- Sequential streaming download (Transmission 4.1+) — watch media while downloading
- RSS auto-download with title-match text/regex rules and dedup
- Labels and sidebar filtering
- Live speed graphs, Statistics panel, and menu-bar popover
- Bandwidth scheduler with turbo/turtle modes by time and day
- Optional per-server mTLS client-certificate authentication
- Multiple servers, Keychain-stored passwords, auto-connect
